name: sqlmc
title: sqlmc
summary: Check all urls of a domain for SQL injections
description:
Check all urls of a domain for SQL injections
SQLMC (SQL Injection Massive Checker) is a tool designed to scan a domain for
SQL injection vulnerabilities. It crawls the given URL up to a specified
depth, checks each link for SQL injection vulnerabilities, and reports its
findings.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sqlmc because Check all urls of a domain for SQL injections.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sqlmc as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqlmc [options] [args]` as documented upstream for sqlmc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sqlmc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sqlmc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
